claim_text: NVIDIA announced multiyear strategic agreements with Lumentum and a $2B investment to grow capacity, advance U.S.-based manufacturing and deepen R&D collaboration in data-center optics.
Lumentum announced a new U.S. manufacturing facility to produce advanced lasers for large AI data centers and said NVIDIA will serve as a customer of the facility.
Lumentum reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of $808.4M, up 90.1% YoY; GAAP gross margin of 44.2%; non-GAAP gross margin of 47.9%; GAAP operating margin of 21.6%; and non-GAAP operating margin of 32.2%.
Q3 FY2026 Components revenue was $533.3M, up 77.3% YoY, and Systems revenue was $275.1M, up 121.1% YoY.
notes: Supports broad-based optical growth rather than one product only.

claim_text: Lumentum management attributed margin expansion to operational rigor, pricing discipline and product mix, including laser chips, pump lasers and narrow linewidth laser assemblies, and said CPO and OCS should increase earnings power as they begin to kick in.
notes: Important bridge from bottleneck to margin, still management language.

claim_text: Lumentum guided Q4 FY2026 revenue to $960M-$1.01B, non-GAAP operating margin to 35%-36% and non-GAAP diluted EPS to $2.85-$3.05.
Lumentum held $3.172B in cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments at Q3 FY2026 end, up $2.017B from Q2, primarily due to Series A Convertible Preferred Stock proceeds.
notes: Liquidity de-risking is real but financing-driven.

claim_text: StockAnalysis showed LITE at $854.96 at the May 29 2026 close, with market cap $66.52B, forward PE 53.97 and trailing PE 162.53.
notes: Third-party market data for valuation-burden context; refresh before live use.

claim_text: The CPO bottleneck recursion trial ranked external lasers and InP laser manufacturing as the first public-company validation path, with LITE listed as a direct laser-source proxy.
notes: Derived method output; not standalone source.

claim_text: The COHR handoff gave watch_only_pending_financial_transmission because COHR has strong NVIDIA validation but weaker cash conversion and lower margins than LITE.
